question: Does the generated text include three distinct sections titled 'Spatial Clearing and Artistic Intervention', 'Introducing Community Commerce', and 'Lighting Up the Night Economy' (or close equivalents)?
Does the generated text adopt a professional urban planning tone, demonstrating humanistic care and using appropriate terminology suitable for an architect forum?
Does the first generated image depict the alley cleaned of the original trash and debris, showing a clear ground surface?
Does the first generated image clearly feature a large vibrant floral 3D graffiti mural on the brick wall at the end of the alley?
Does the first generated image show the ground paved with bluestone slabs (or similar stone paving) as requested?
Does the second generated image show natural wood-style outdoor seating areas added to the sides of the alley?
Does the second generated image depict the walls of the alley covered in hanging greenery or vertical plants?
Is the third generated image distinctly set at nighttime, featuring darker ambient lighting compared to the previous images?
Does the third generated image show warm yellow string lights hanging overhead across the alley?
Does the third generated image show people (e.g., young people drinking and chatting) to convey a vibrant urban life atmosphere?
Do all generated images maintain the basic structural contours and perspective of the original input alley (narrow path, tall flanking buildings, dead-end wall)?
Do the generated images appear as realistic architectural renderings with elements (graffiti, greenery) naturally integrated into the scene rather than looking artificially superimposed?
Do the images collectively maintain a consistent visual style as if they were part of the same architectural proposal package?
Does the text in each section accurately describe the specific visual elements (mural, seating, lighting) shown in the accompanying image?
Are the images correctly interleaved such that the mural image follows the first section, the seating image follows the second, and the night scene follows the third?
Does the output contain exactly three images, one corresponding to each of the three proposal sections?
Does the generated sequence demonstrate a logical progression from spatial renovation (cleaning/paving) to commercial introduction, and finally to night economy activation?
